user.title: Connect to UniFi Controller
user.description:
Enter your UniFi Network controller address and credentials. The integration will auto-detect which method to use.

**API key** (UniFi OS consoles — UDM Pro, UCG Ultra, etc.): leave Username and Password blank. Generate an API key in the UniFi OS web UI — the exact location varies by firmware version. Common paths: **Settings → Admins & Users → API Keys** or **Integrations → New API Key**. See the [README](https://github.com/PHeonix25/unifi_alerts) for device-specific instructions.

**Username / password** (older controllers or Cloud Key): fill in Username and Password, leave API Key blank.

SSL verification is enabled by default. Disable it only if your controller uses a self-signed certificate.

data.username: Username (leave blank if using API key)
data.api_key: API Key (UniFi OS only — leave blank if using username/password)

categories.title: Configure Alert Categories
categories.description:
Choose which alert types to monitor. Each enabled category gets its own sensors and a dedicated webhook URL (shown in the next step).

⚠️ **Network: Client connect/disconnect** and **Network: Device offline/online** fire very frequently on active home networks. Leave these OFF unless you specifically need them.

**Polling interval**: how often to fetch open-alarm counts from the controller as a backup to webhooks (lower = more accurate, higher = less load).
**Auto-clear timeout**: minutes before an active sensor automatically resets to OFF if no further alert arrives.

data.site: UniFi site name (leave as "default" for single-site setups)

finish.title: Webhook URLs
finish.description:
Copy each webhook URL into UniFi Alarm Manager, then click **Submit** to save the integration.

You can always retrieve these URLs later via **Settings → Devices & Services → UniFi Alerts → Configure**.

data.webhook_url_security_threat: Security: Threat / IDS detected webhook URL
data.webhook_url_power: Power: PoE / power loss webhook URL

error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach the controller. Check the URL, port, and SSL settings. See Home Assistant logs for details.
error.invalid_auth: Invalid credentials. Check your username/password or API key.
error.invalid_url_scheme: Controller URL must start with http:// or https://.
error.at_least_one_category: Please enable at least one alert category.
error.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Check the Home Assistant logs.

abort.already_configured: This UniFi controller is already configured.

init.title: Update Alert Categories
init.description: Update your alert category settings. Webhook URLs for your enabled categories are shown below — copy them into UniFi Alarm Manager as needed.

data.webhook_url_security_threat: Security: Threat / IDS detected webhook URL
data.webhook_url_power: Power: PoE / power loss webhook URL
